claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current circumstances.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's doing his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or does he live with anyone else who might be claiming for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. He lives with me and nobody else is claiming it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Even though Alex is 18, you still qualify because he is under 20 and is staying in approved education (his A-levels at sixth form college). Since you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

**Important note:** Your Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20. If his circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education, starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in his own right), you should contact the Child Benefit Office.

Also, please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as he is under 20 and in approved education. You live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Alex meets this criteria as he is under 20 and in approved education. You live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
